string 2: Those Digimon—
I'll have Dr. Simmons explain the situation here.
She should be just up ahead.
M-Merukimon...!
...The doctor told me everything.
It has been some time. I am gladdened to see you are all safe.
But... what happened to you? Why are you in this world?

We... lost our war with the Titans. With no other avenue of
escape open, my allies and I fled here.

We are in a awful state, with no means of contacting those who
remained in Iliad, even to let them know we are safe.
O-Oh, wow...
But isn't it... hard for you to live here?

Indeed, it is. Many humans here look upon us as the enemy.
And it is true that we have caused considerable damage.
But how did it get this way...?
Truth be told, human reasoning eludes me.

Naturally, I am aware that there are others like yourselves
who are capable of coexisting with Digimon...

However, it would appear the greater part of humanity is
loath to do so.
But nothing good ever comes of conflict! We always lose more than we gain!

You may be right. Yet the issue of fact is that we find
ourselves besieged by two threats.

If we fail to face either of them, we will have no future.
Thus we must risk our very existence to combat this crisis.
Sounds like you're in a do or die situation.

While the history and circumstances seem to differ between
my world and the one there...
...there's likely one single fact that remains the same.
The Shinjuku Inferno is sparked by an intense conflict.
And you're right in the middle of it.

You have to put a stop to this war. Do everything in your
power to bring all three sides to a ceasefire.

I'll come right out and say it. This is the only way to
prevent the end of the world.
...Yes? What is it?
Fighting won't solve anything.{next}
Isn't there any way to avoid conflict?{next}
Infighting among Digimon will destroy the world.{next}
Isn't there some way forward that doesn't involve war?

...We have suffered injury and great loss. This is a battle
to get back what we have lost.

Even if I were to forbid fighting, I doubt many would
heed my words.
Thus I cannot do so without demanding conditions.

I am not saying I want to reclaim everything, but at the
very least... I would ask that our major villages be freed.

If this could be accomplished with words alone, then there
could be nothing better.

And if we are able to ascertain that even some of the Titans
have withdrawn, a ceasefire could be an option.
We'll go negotiate the return of the conquered villages!{next}
Encouraging a Titan retreat is my next mission then!{next}
We'll stop the fighting and prevent the Shinjuku Inferno!{next}
Yeah, good call.
Let's get over there and get those villages freed from Titan control.

First up... we need to talk to the Titans. That's
the best way to do this without violence.
I'm a representative working to stop a war in another world—an agent.{next}
Indeed... I can only hope for your success.

The gate is still traversable, but you will not find it the
same as it was eight years ago.
Do take care of yourselves.
W-Wait!
...What is it?

If you're going to the Digital World for us, can I ask
another favor of you?
Several settlements survived the Titan invasion...

...and I hear that in Central Town there's a Digimon who's a
skilled healer.
So what do you want with this Digimon?

Apparently, this healer isn't picky about which patients to
treat... Even treats Titans, they say.

I know someone who needs those skills. If you come across
this healer, could you relay the message?
You mean there's a Digimon you want healed?
...Yeah.
It's Lord Merukimon.

Despite trying to keep up appearances, the wounds our lord
sustained are severe...
So I'm begging you...!
Okay. We'll try to find this healer.
...Sorry for the extra trouble... and thank you!
